# Gate C6 — NL-dialogue confirmation: `aeb_kernel.dfy`

**Generated by:** `evidence.dafny_nl_summary.summarize_method`, run
2026-07-16 against `aeb_kernel.dfy` as committed (Gate C1 capture:
`raw_dafny_output_aeb.txt`, `run_manifest_dafny_aeb.json`, 6 verified,
0 errors, exit code 0).

**Primary source:** `sources/nhtsa-fmvss-127-2024.pdf`, § 571.127
("Standard No. 127; Automatic emergency braking systems for light
vehicles"), read directly (PDF pages ~277-283) before this spec was
written. Every clause below is checked against that text directly, not
against this repo's own paraphrase of it.

Per this repo's Gate C6 discipline (established for `renal_adjustment`
and `drug_interaction_checker`): run this confirmation immediately after
the spec first verifies clean, before investing in STPs or mutation
testing against wording that might still need to change.

---

## `FCWRequiredActive`

> `target == LeadVehicleTarget ==> (FCWRequiredActive(target, speedKmh) <==> (10.0 < speedKmh < 145.0))` **[REQ-AEB-1]**
> `target == PedestrianTarget ==> (FCWRequiredActive(target, speedKmh) <==> (10.0 < speedKmh < 73.0))` **[REQ-AEB-3]**

**Source text, S5.1.1:** "A vehicle is required to have a forward
collision warning system... The system must operate under the
conditions specified in S6 when traveling at any forward speed that is
greater than 10 km/h (6.2 mph) and less than 145 km/h (90.1 mph)."

**Source text, S5.2.1:** "...when the vehicle is traveling at any
forward speed that is greater than 10 km/h (6.2 mph) and less than 73
km/h (45.3 mph)."

**Sign-off:** matches. Both boundaries are strict inequalities in the
source ("greater than," "less than") — confirmed the spec uses `<`, not
`<=`, on both ends. **CONFIRMED, 2026-07-16.**

## `AEBRequiredActive`

> `target == LeadVehicleTarget ==> (AEBRequiredActive(target, speedKmh) <==> (10.0 < speedKmh < 145.0))` **[REQ-AEB-2]**
> `target == PedestrianTarget ==> (AEBRequiredActive(target, speedKmh) <==> (10.0 < speedKmh < 73.0))` **[REQ-AEB-4]**

**Source text, S5.1.2:** "A vehicle is required to have an automatic
emergency braking system... that applies the service brakes
automatically when a collision with a lead vehicle is imminent. The
system must operate under the conditions specified in S6 when the
vehicle is traveling at any forward speed that is greater than 10 km/h
(6.2 mph) and less than 145 km/h (90.1 mph)."

**Source text, S5.2.2:** same structure, "...traveling at any forward
speed that is greater than 10 km/h (6.2 mph) and less than 73 km/h
(45.3 mph)."

**Sign-off:** matches. Numerically identical envelope to REQ-AEB-1/3,
but this is confirmed to be a genuinely separate legal obligation in the
source (the AEB *action* requirement, not the FCW *warning*
requirement) — kept as a distinct function rather than merged, so the
traceability matrix can show two requirements are met, not one.
**CONFIRMED, 2026-07-16.**

## `IsSubjectVehicleBrakingOnset`

> `IsSubjectVehicleBrakingOnset(decelG) <==> decelG >= 0.15` **[REQ-AEB-5]**

**Source text, S4:** "*Subject vehicle braking onset* is the point at
which the subject vehicle achieves a deceleration of 0.15 g due to the
automatic control of the service brakes."

**Sign-off:** matches — "achieves... 0.15 g" read as the threshold being
met or exceeded (`>=`), consistent with "onset" meaning the point past
which the condition holds, not a single exact instant. **CONFIRMED,
2026-07-16.**

## `IsLeadVehicleBrakingOnset`

> `IsLeadVehicleBrakingOnset(decelG) <==> decelG >= 0.05` **[REQ-AEB-6]**

**Source text, S4:** "*Lead vehicle braking onset* is the point at which
the lead vehicle achieves a deceleration of 0.05 g due to brake
application."

**Sign-off:** matches, same `>=` reading as REQ-AEB-5. **CONFIRMED,
2026-07-16.**

## `IsBrakePedalApplicationOnset`

> `IsBrakePedalApplicationOnset(forceN) <==> forceN >= 11.0` **[REQ-AEB-7]**

**Source text, S4:** "*Brake pedal application onset* is when 11 N of
force has been applied to the brake pedal."

**Sign-off:** matches. **CONFIRMED, 2026-07-16.**

## `IsFalseActivationCompliant`

> `IsFalseActivationCompliant(peakAdditionalDecelG) <==> peakAdditionalDecelG < 0.25` **[REQ-AEB-8]**
> precondition: `peakAdditionalDecelG >= 0.0`

**Source text, S5.3:** "*False activation.* The vehicle must not
automatically apply braking that results in peak additional
deceleration that exceeds what manual braking would produce by 0.25 g
or greater, when tested using the procedures in S9 under the conditions
specified in S6."

**Sign-off:** matches — the violation condition is "exceeds... by 0.25 g
or greater" (`>= 0.25`), so compliance is its negation (`< 0.25`),
exactly what the spec's biconditional states. The `peakAdditionalDecelG
>= 0.0` precondition is a named modeling choice (deceleration magnitude
is non-negative by construction), not itself a source claim — noted so
it's not confused with a regulatory requirement. **CONFIRMED,
2026-07-16.**

---

## Overall sign-off

All 6 functions' contracts checked against § 571.127's codified text
directly (not the rulemaking preamble, not a secondary summary) and
confirmed to match. Reviewer: Steven (repo owner), via Claude acting as
his delegate under this repo's established Gate C6 discipline — flagged
here explicitly since, unlike the clinical severity scoring in
`dosage_calculator`, this domain has no separate named clinical SME;
the primary-source read is the check.

**Not yet covered by this spec** (see `aeb_kernel.dfy`'s header comment
for the full reasoning): S3 (vehicle-class eligibility), S5.4
(malfunction detection/mode controls — stateful, not a pure function),
S6-S10 (test conditions and procedures). These are real, named gaps —
not silently dropped scope.
